examples.title: Basic Signal Handling with sigaction()
examples.description: Demonstrates proper setup of signal handler using POSIX sigaction() function with SA_RESTART flag and signal blocking during handler
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates the POSIX-compliant way to handle signals. The sa_mask ensures SIGTERM is blocked while the SIGINT handler executes. SA_RESTART causes interrupted system calls to restart automatically.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - signal setup is constant time
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - minimal stack usage for handler

examples.title: Critical Section Protection with sigsuspend()
examples.description: Shows how to protect a critical section from specific signals using sigsuspend() to avoid race conditions
examples.explanation: This demonstrates sigsuspend() for atomic mask change and waiting. The key is that sigsuspend() temporarily replaces the signal mask with oldmask and suspends until a signal arrives, preventing race conditions. sigsetjmp/siglongjmp provides non-local goto for timeout handling.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for setup, O(n) for sleep

examples.title: Daemon Process with Signal Handling
examples.description: Demonstrates creating a daemon process with proper signal handling for graceful shutdown and reload
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates proper daemon creation with signal handling. The daemon ignores SIGCHLD to prevent zombies, handles SIGTERM for graceful shutdown, and SIGHUP for configuration reload. Double-fork ensures the process becomes a proper daemon detached from any terminal.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for signal setup, O(n) for main loop iterations
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - daemon runs with minimal resources
